tiaa-design-system.title: TIAA Design System
tiaa-design-system.context: TIAA operates across multiple web and mobile products serving diverse user groups. The design system initiative aimed to unify design standards, improve consistency, and support scalable product development.
responsibilities: Led the design system initiative across web and mobile products
Managed and mentored designers contributing to the system
Partnered with engineering and product leadership on governance and adoption
tiaa-design-system.problem: Inconsistent patterns and duplicated effort across teams slowed development and reduced experience quality. The challenge was creating a system that balanced flexibility with governance.
decisions: Defined clear component ownership and contribution models
Established documentation standards to support adoption
Designed components to scale across multiple product teams without fragmenting ownership
Prioritized long-term maintainability over short-term convenience
tiaa-design-system.outcome: Delivered a scalable design system that improved consistency, reduced duplication, and enabled more efficient product development across teams.

images.alt: Component variants across themes and contexts
Design system documentation and governance structure

unitedhealth-transportation.title: Transportation Benefits Experience
unitedhealth-transportation.context: This work focused on the transportation benefits feature within the UnitedHealth Group member experience, allowing eligible users to schedule transportation for medical appointments. The experience needed to support accessibility, clarity, and trust.
responsibilities: Led UX/UI design for transportation scheduling workflows
Designed user flows for eligibility, booking, and confirmation
Collaborated with cross-functional teams in a regulated healthcare environment
unitedhealth-transportation.problem: Users needed to schedule transportation reliably and with confidence, often under time-sensitive or stressful conditions. Existing flows lacked clarity and required unnecessary steps.
decisions: Simplified scheduling flows to reduce user effort
Emphasized clarity and confirmation at each step
Designed with accessibility requirements and edge cases in mind
Reduced cognitive load by limiting choices at critical decision points
unitedhealth-transportation.outcome: Delivered a clearer, more accessible transportation scheduling experience that improved usability and user confidence.

nutrient-website.title: Nutrient Website
nutrient-website.context: Nutrient is a document processing and SDK platform used by developers and enterprise teams. The project involved consolidating five separate product websites and brands into a single unified platform. The website needed to clearly communicate complex technical capabilities, support multiple product offerings, and scale as the platform evolved. The goal was to create a product-led website that balanced clarity, performance, and technical depth.
responsibilities: Led UX/UI design for the entire website experience
Designed and implemented the site using React and Astro
Partnered with product and marketing to align messaging with product capabilities
Ensured performance, accessibility, and maintainability in the build
nutrient-website.problem: The website needed to explain a technically sophisticated product without overwhelming users. Existing content struggled to clearly differentiate offerings and guide users toward relevant information. The challenge was designing a structure that worked for both technical and non-technical audiences while remaining flexible as the product expanded.
decisions: Designed the site with a product-first information architecture rather than a marketing-first approach
Made clarity the primary constraint, surfacing complexity only when it added value
Built reusable layout and content patterns to support ongoing iteration
Chose Astro to optimize performance while integrating React where interactivity was required
Treated accessibility and page performance as non-negotiable design constraints
nutrient-website.outcome: Delivered a fast, product-led website that clearly communicates Nutrient's offerings and provides a scalable foundation for future growth.

images.alt: Product-led home experience with clear positioning
Product-level navigation designed to scale across offerings

lowes-pro-supply.title: Lowe's Pro Supply Application
lowes-pro-supply.context: Lowe's Pro Supply supports professional contractors managing inventory, ordering, and fulfillment. The application serves users operating in fast-paced, operational environments where efficiency and clarity are critical.
responsibilities: Led UX/UI design for core application workflows
Designed interfaces for professional, task-focused users
Collaborated with product and engineering teams to align on feasibility and scope
lowes-pro-supply.problem: Existing workflows required excessive navigation and context switching, slowing down common tasks. The challenge was simplifying complex operational flows while preserving flexibility for professional users with varied needs.
decisions: Reduced navigation depth by consolidating related actions into focused views
Intentionally prioritized speed and scannability over visual detail to support repeat usage
Designed interfaces optimized for repeat workflows rather than first-time discovery
Focused on consistency across workflows to reduce training and cognitive overhead
lowes-pro-supply.outcome: Improved workflow efficiency and predictability for professional users, enabling faster task completion across common operational flows.

synquery-product.title: Synquery Product and Website
synquery-product.role: UX/UI Design, Website Design and Development
synquery-product.context: Synquery is an expert network platform designed to connect organizations with specialized expertise. The work included defining the product experience as well as designing and developing the public-facing website.
responsibilities: Led product UX/UI design for the core platform
Designed and developed the marketing website
Worked directly with founders on product definition and positioning
Balanced speed, clarity, and scalability in early-stage decisions
synquery-product.problem: As an early-stage product, Synquery needed a clear and credible experience without overbuilding. The challenge was defining strong foundations that could evolve as the product matured.
decisions: Focused on core workflows rather than edge-case functionality
Designed flexible patterns that could scale without rework
Aligned product and website design to present a cohesive narrative
Prioritized clarity over feature density in early releases
synquery-product.outcome: Delivered a cohesive product and web experience that established clear foundations while allowing for rapid iteration as the platform evolved.
